summary: Linked the Kirby Yoshi capture object by shrinking `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` down to the small matching range at 0x8010A930-0x8010AA64 and moving the remaining nonmatching Yoshi egg code into a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`/`.h` split. The build config now marks `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` as `Matching` and adds `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` as `NonMatching`; GALE01 splits and symbols were updated to reflect the new text/sdata2 boundaries and local float labels. Related includes and references were adjusted to use `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` where the moved egg routines are needed.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` and added `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` as a new `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` split the old `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` range from `.text 0x8010A930-0x8010D740` and `.sdata2 0x804D9560-0x804D95B0` into `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` at `.text 0x8010A930-0x8010AA64`, `.sdata2 0x804D9560-0x804D9568`, plus new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` at `.text 0x8010AA64-0x8010D740`, `.sdata2 0x804D9568-0x804D95B0`.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` converted several early `.sdata2` float symbols around 0x804D9558-0x804D9564 from global `ftKb_Init_804D...` labels to local `@...` labels.

Most of the previous contents of `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` were removed from that file and reintroduced in the new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`, while `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` retained only the leading capture-Yoshi functions visible in the excerpt, including `fn_8010A930` and `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AA2C`.
`ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` was removed and replaced by a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` header carrying declarations for the moved code.
`ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c`, `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c`, `itpeachtoad.c`, and `ftmotionstates.c` received small one-line include/reference adjustments consistent with the new header and split.
smart_moves: Instead of trying to force the entire former `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` blob to match, the PR isolated the already-matchable leading slice and moved the rest into a separate nonmatching object. This allowed immediate linkage progress without blocking on the larger Yoshi egg code range.

The source split mirrors the address boundary exactly: `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` ends at 0x8010AA64 and `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` starts at 0x8010AA64, preserving object order and making the split searchable in both `splits.txt` and `configure.py`.
Updating `.sdata2` boundaries alongside `.text` prevented constants from being attributed to the wrong object after the split.
Replacing broad includes in `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` with only the headers needed by the small matching slice reduced dependency noise and helped clarify what the linked object actually uses.
Renaming the moved code around the visible `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*` routines into `ftkirbyyoshiegg` made the remaining nonmatching file name better match its dominant behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a large nonmatching file contains an early contiguous range that can link, split the file at the real linker boundary and mark only that range `Matching`; keep the remainder as a new `NonMatching` object.
A successful source split requires synchronized edits across `configure.py`, `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, source files, headers, and any include sites.
For object-split PRs, verify both `.text` and `.sdata2` ranges. Constants near the split boundary may need symbol-scope changes from global synthetic names to local labels.

Header ownership should follow the moved declarations. If downstream files use Yoshi egg routines rather than capture-Yoshi routines, include `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` rather than preserving the old capture header name.

Large code moves can be evidence of a mechanical resplit rather than logic changes; future reviewers should inspect boundaries and build config first before treating such diffs as behavioral rewrites.
naming_conventions: `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` is used for the small linked capture-Yoshi object at 0x8010A930-0x8010AA64.
`ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` and `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` name the moved nonmatching object containing Kirby/Yoshi egg routines such as `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*`.
Synthetic global float names of the form `ftKb_Init_804D....` may be replaced with local `@NNN` symbols when the constants are local to a linked object.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` after reducing it to the matching address range.
Introduced a new nonmatching object for the unmatched remainder to avoid regressing object order while still linking the first segment.
Used exact address-range partitioning in `splits.txt`: old `0x8010A930-0x8010D740` became `0x8010A930-0x8010AA64` plus `0x8010AA64-0x8010D740`.
Adjusted `.sdata2` partitioning from the former combined range to `0x804D9560-0x804D9568` for `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` and `0x804D9568-0x804D95B0` for `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`.
Trimmed unused includes and static forward declarations from the now-small matching source file.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that link or rename `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`.
Check whether `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*` routines later became matching and whether the file stayed split from `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c`.
Search for other PRs converting `ftKb_Init_804D...` symbols to local `@...` labels around `.sdata2` split boundaries.
Review follow-up include cleanup in `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c`, item files, and `ftmotionstates.c` after the new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` header.
